purpose: Defines the Standing Authority (SA) — a bounded, owner-issued authority from which a scoped governance-mutation authorization is DERIVED automatically at merge-check time, instead of requiring a manual per-PR GMA. One issuance -> many bounded derivations. This is the bridge from governed demo (per-mutation authorization) to governed operations (policy-bound authorization).
canonical_invariant: If no valid object exists -> nothing happens.
compressed_rule: Manual GMA governs one mutation. Standing Authority governs a bounded class of mutations. Both must end in proof.

relationship_to_gma.manual_gma: Per-mutation authorization: one governed PR -> one owner workflow_dispatch -> one GMA entry. Safe, explicit, expensive. Remains the path for rare/one-off governance mutations.
relationship_to_gma.standing_authority: Policy-bound authorization: owner issues a bounded authority once; every fitting PR derives a scoped authorization automatically. Required for continuous governed action.
relationship_to_gma.invariant_preserved: Authority is issued once and bounded, not weakened. No valid authority -> no governance mutation still holds.

issuance_model.authority_before_execution: Owner workflow_dispatch is the preexisting authority (per governance/recursive/RECURSIVE_GOVERNANCE_CONTAINMENT_MODEL.json). Implicit authority is forbidden.
issuance_model.no_gma_required_to_issue: The SA registry lives under governance/authorizations/**, which is exempt from governed-file classification (authorization evidence != governance primitive). Issuing a SA therefore needs no GMA and introduces no bootstrap circularity.
issuance_model.default_scope_guidance: V1 issues narrow by default — one mutation class / one surface (e.g. claude/* x workflow_mutation x .github/workflows/** x 24h x <=2 merges). Reusable authority should start narrow and widen only after the path is exercised.

format: JSON Lines — append-only. One immutable standing_authority record per line; revocations are appended as standing_authority_revocation records (no deletion or mutation of prior lines).
append_only_enforcement: merge-governance-check asserts the base-branch version of standing_authority_registry.jsonl is a byte-exact PREFIX of the head version (same guarantee as gma_registry.jsonl, Issue #1984). Existing entries may never be modified or removed.

standing_authority_record.authority_hash_input: sha256 of JSON{branch_pattern, sorted mutation_classes, sorted path_globs, max_merges, ttl_hours, issued_at} — repo-verifiable, no external runtime

example.intent: Claude may modify workflows on claude/* for 24h up to 2 merges

revocation_record.semantics: Append-only revocation. A standing_authority whose authority_id is cited by any standing_authority_revocation record is non-selectable for derivation, regardless of its TTL.

admission_tiers: Tier 1: explicit per-PR GMA in gma_registry.jsonl, keyed by (branch, governed_files_hash).
Tier 2: legacy singleton GOVERNANCE_MUTATION_AUTHORIZATION.json, keyed by governed_files_hash (transitional fallback).
Tier 3: Standing Authority derivation — if no explicit GMA matches, evaluate the governed diff against the SA registry.
authorized_iff_all: every detected governance_mutation/workflow_mutation class is in bounds.mutation_classes  (what)
every governed file matches >=1 bounds.path_globs  (scope containment — nothing outside the bounded surface)
consumed budget < bounds.max_merges  (async proof-ledger budget — see bound_model)
derivation_rule.tie_break: If multiple SAs qualify, the most-recently-issued is chosen (deterministic).
derivation_rule.base_branch_provenance: Tier 3 derives ONLY from authorities present on the PR's base branch (read from BASE_SHA), never from the PR checkout. governance/authorizations/** is exempt from governed-file detection and the append-only guard only proves entries were appended, so reading the head registry would let an ordinary PR append a broad authority and self-authorize — minting a reusable authority outside the owner-dispatch issuance model. A Standing Authority must therefore first land on the base branch through governed admission before it can authorize any PR. The budget ledger is likewise read from the base branch so a PR cannot rewrite it to free budget.
derivation_rule.verifier_integrity: The derivation module (runtime/standing-authority.mjs) is loaded from the BASE branch (sa_verifier_base.mjs, extracted from BASE_SHA), never the PR checkout. runtime/*.mjs is not a governed path, so importing the head version would run the PR's own (possibly rigged) authorization code to decide whether the PR is authorized. Both the merge gate (Tier 3) and the merge-proof attribution step run the trusted, already-merged verifier.
derivation_rule.appended_record_validation: When a PR modifies standing_authority_registry.jsonl, every newly appended record is validated for shape and internal consistency (required fields present, source_authority === OWNER_WORKFLOW_DISPATCH, status === STANDING_AUTHORITY_VALID, well-formed bounds, expires_at after issued_at, and authority_hash recomputed from the bounds via the BASE verifier). A malformed or internally inconsistent record fails the merge as MERGE_LEGITIMACY_NULL. This raises the bar against forged authorities, but is NOT cryptographic issuance provenance: signing is post-V1, so a reviewer must still vet that an appended authority was legitimately issued by the owner-dispatch workflow.
derivation_rule.path_containment_scope: Standing Authority path_globs containment is applied ONLY to files whose mutation requires GMA authorization (governance_mutation / workflow_mutation). proof_persistence (merge_proof_registry.jsonl) and non-gated classes (runtime/schema/migration/deployment_config) are excluded, so an in-scope PR that also touches such a file is not falsely declined.
derivation_rule.budget_attribution: merge-proof.yml stamps the authority by reproducing the gate's selection exactly: it reads the SA registry, proof ledger and verifier from the same BASE_SHA, applies the REAL proof ledger so a budget-exhausted authority is skipped just as the gate skipped it, and evaluates TTL at the merge timestamp (merged_at) — not proof-generation time — so an authority that authorized a merge but expires shortly after still records its budget consumption. Budget is counted only from well-formed proof_entry records with a unique proof_id (forged or duplicate ledger lines do not consume budget).

bound_model.ttl: HARD bound. Synchronous and exact: now < expires_at is evaluated at merge-check time.
bound_model.max_merges: Async proof-ledger BUDGET. Best-effort, observed via the append-only merge_proof_registry.jsonl.
bound_model.statement: V1 standing authority is TTL-hard-bound and proof-budget-observed. It is not strictly serial-budget-enforced until a merge queue or synchronous proof reservation exists.
bound_model.rationale: Proof persistence is async (a separate proof-registry/* PR), so the budget count lags; concurrent PRs under one SA can briefly overshoot max_merges. TTL bounds the blast radius regardless. This matches the existing system's async-proof consistency model — it is not a regression and the system does NOT claim strict serial replay safety under concurrent PRs.

proof_binding.requirement: Every governed merge derived under a SA must end in a merge proof. merge-proof.yml stamps standing_authority_id (and governed_files_hash) into the proof's canonical_payload and into the appended merge_proof_registry.jsonl entry.
proof_binding.effect: The append-only proof ledger IS the budget-consumption ledger. proof_must_follow_execution is preserved.

invariant_mapping.AUTHORIZED: SA preexists via owner dispatch; implicit authority forbidden.
invariant_mapping.BOUNDED: branch_pattern AND mutation_classes AND path_globs AND ttl_hours AND max_merges.
invariant_mapping.REPLAY_SAFE: branch-pattern binding; out-of-scope/cross-branch diffs not derivable; budget consumed via append-only, nonce-bound proofs. Strict serial replay safety is NOT claimed under concurrent PRs (see bound_model).
invariant_mapping.PROOF_BOUND: admission still requires the downstream merge proof, which stamps standing_authority_id.
invariant_mapping.fail_closed: no explicit GMA and no covering SA -> MERGE_LEGITIMACY_NULL.

known_limitations: max_merges is best-effort (async proof-ledger budget), not strictly serial-enforced — see bound_model. Strict serialization requires a merge queue (currently DISABLED_FOR_GOVERNED_BRANCHES per CI-003) or synchronous proof reservation.

Tier 3 widens what can merge without per-PR human action; mitigated by owner-only issuance, narrow default scope, short TTL, append-only + revocable authorities, and the merge gate remaining the single enforcement point.

Issuance provenance is not cryptographically proven in V1: appended_record_validation checks shape and internal consistency, but a well-formed authority that was hand-crafted rather than emitted by the owner-dispatch workflow would pass structural validation. The base-branch landing requirement means such a record must still be merged through reviewed, governed admission, so review is the V1 provenance control. Signed Standing Authorities are sequenced for post-V1.
Budget consumption counts well-formed proof_entry records by unique proof_id, but does not yet verify proof_hash against the canonical payload; full proof verification is post-V1.
out_of_scope_v1: Cryptographic signatures on SAs (V1 stays repo-verifiable deterministic).
